roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: 4 milestones: Ritika is a beginner with 0 experience and a wide gap from student to Junior Frontend Developer. 14 hours/week and low confidence necessitate smaller, safer steps to build foundational programming, system interaction, application building, and job readiness skills gradually.

vision_profile.current_state: A final-year B.Sc Math student with no professional experience, seeking to enter the tech industry.
vision_profile.main_blocker: Lack of foundational frontend development skills (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and fear of competition from peers with more resources.
vision_profile.top_motivation: Achieving financial independence to support her family before her younger brother starts college.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: You can write basic programs, manage code with version control, and understand the fundamental building blocks of software development, ready to explore web-specific technologies.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are needed to cover foundational programming logic, basic data handling, and essential collaboration tools for any aspiring developer.
milestones.identity_statement: You are building the core programming muscles that will power your future web applications, taking your first confident steps into the world of development.

project.title: Personal Digital Resume Page
project.description: Design and implement a simple, static personal resume web page. You will structure content, apply basic styling, and ensure it's version-controlled. This project focuses on applying foundational programming logic (e.g., for dynamic content generation if applicable) and setting up a professional development workflow.
project.deliverable: A deployed static website URL and a link to the GitHub repository containing the project code.
seeded_errors: The deployed page has broken links due to incorrect relative paths in the HTML structure.

modules.title: Python for AI
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are sufficient to cover the absolute basics of programming syntax, control flow, and function definition, essential for any coding journey.

scenario.title: Debugging a Simple Python Script
Optimizing Loop Performance
Refactoring for Modularity

modules.title: Algorithms & Data Handling
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are essential to grasp fundamental data structures and basic problem-solving techniques, which are critical for managing and manipulating data in any application.

scenario.title: Choosing the Right Data Structure for User Data
Optimizing Data Retrieval for a Large Dataset
Identifying the Root Cause of a Program Crash

science.desc: A critical function in your program is returning incorrect results for certain inputs, but only intermittently. You need to identify the logical flaw and fix it under pressure.

modules.title: Collaboration & Tools
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are crucial for understanding version control, collaborative workflows, and basic code quality, which are non-negotiable for any professional developer.

scenario.title: Recovering Lost Commits in Git
Resolving a Complex Merge Conflict
Reviewing a Peer's Pull Request for Clarity

science.desc: Explain the importance of version control in a team environment and describe a typical Git workflow for contributing to a project.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: You can design structured code, interact with external APIs, and understand network communication, enabling you to build web pages that fetch and display dynamic data.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are crucial to transition from basic programming to building interactive web components, focusing on code structure, API integration, and underlying network principles.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now learning how web applications 'talk' and organize themselves, transforming static pages into dynamic, responsive experiences.

project.title: Dynamic Recipe Finder
project.description: Build a web application that fetches recipe data from a public API (e.g., TheMealDB API) and displays it dynamically. Users should be able to search for recipes and view details. This project emphasizes API consumption, dynamic content rendering, and structured code design.
project.deliverable: A deployed web application URL, GitHub repository, and a brief architectural overview document.
seeded_errors: The application fails to display images from the API due to incorrect URL parsing or CORS issues.

modules.title: OOP & SOLID Principles
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are fundamental for writing maintainable, scalable, and understandable code, which is vital for larger frontend applications.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Procedural Script into OOP
Applying OCP to a UI Component
Implementing Observer for UI Events

modules.title: API Design & Service Layer
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are essential for understanding how to interact with backend services, which is the backbone of most dynamic web applications.

skills.title: Introduction to RESTful APIs
lessons: What is an API and REST?

scenario.title: Designing API Endpoints for a Blog Application
Debugging a Failed API Call in Production
Securing a Public API Key in a Frontend App

science.desc: A user reports that your web application is showing outdated data even after refreshing. You suspect a caching issue or an API problem. Diagnose and resolve the data inconsistency.

modules.title: Networking & Communication
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are necessary to understand the fundamental protocols and tools that govern how web applications communicate, providing a deeper insight into web development.

scenario.title: Diagnosing a 404 Error in a Web Application

video.title: The Role of DNS in Web Access

scenario.title: Troubleshooting a Website Not Resolving
Diagnosing a Frontend Rendering Issue with DevTools

science.desc: Describe the journey of a web request from the browser to the server and back, explaining the role of DNS and HTTP along the way.

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: You can build robust and performant web application components, implement testing strategies, and understand distributed system concepts, preparing you for complex frontend development.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are necessary to advance from basic interactivity to building scalable, testable, and modular frontend applications, covering performance, quality assurance, and architectural thinking.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now architecting and optimizing web applications, ensuring they are fast, reliable, and easy to maintain, just like a seasoned builder.

project.title: Interactive E-commerce Product Catalog
project.description: Develop a multi-page e-commerce product catalog with client-side routing, basic state management (e.g., for a shopping cart), and user authentication (mock). Focus on component reusability, performance, and robust error handling. Integrate with a mock API for product data.
project.deliverable: A deployed web application URL, GitHub repository, and a document outlining performance optimizations and testing strategy.
seeded_errors: The shopping cart state is not persistent across page navigations, leading to lost items.

modules.title: Scalability & Performance
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are critical for building high-performing web applications that provide a smooth user experience, covering optimization techniques and asynchronous patterns.

scenario.title: Improving LCP for a Slow-Loading Hero Image
Implementing a Service Worker for a PWA
Preventing UI Freezes with Web Workers

modules.title: Testing, Logging & DevOps Foundations
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are essential for ensuring the quality, reliability, and maintainability of frontend applications through systematic testing and effective debugging.

scenario.title: Writing Unit Tests for a Complex UI Widget
Debugging a Failed Integration Test for User Login
Diagnosing a Production Bug from User Reports and Logs

science.desc: A critical user flow in your application is intermittently failing in production, but passes all local tests. You need to analyze production logs and user reports to pinpoint the elusive bug.

modules.title: Microservices Architecture
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are important for understanding how large applications are broken down into smaller, manageable parts, which directly applies to component-based frontend development.

scenario.title: Designing a Flexible Card Component for Multiple Uses
Centralizing State for a Complex Form
Designing Communication Between Independent UI Widgets

science.desc: Explain how you would break down a complex user interface into smaller, reusable components, and how these components would communicate with each other.

milestones.label: M04
milestones.o: You are ready to apply for entry-level frontend developer roles, having built and deployed a portfolio project, and prepared for technical interviews.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are essential for consolidating all learned skills, deploying a professional portfolio, and preparing comprehensively for the job market, including interviews.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now a job-ready Junior Frontend Developer, equipped with a strong portfolio and the confidence to tackle real-world challenges and secure your first role.

project.title: Personalized Dashboard Application
project.description: Build and deploy a personalized dashboard web application that integrates data from multiple (mock) sources (e.g., weather, news, to-do list). This capstone project requires you to apply all learned skills, including API integration, component design, state management, performance considerations, and robust error handling. The goal is a polished, deployable application for your portfolio.
project.deliverable: A fully deployed web application URL, a comprehensive GitHub repository, a detailed project README, and a short presentation video.
seeded_errors: The dashboard frequently crashes when fetching data from one of the external APIs due to unhandled network errors or malformed responses.

modules.title: Cloud & Deployment
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are crucial for taking a web application from local development to a live, accessible product, covering hosting, automation, and basic monitoring.

skills.title: Static Site Hosting & CDNs
lessons: Options for Static Site Deployment (e.g., Netlify, Vercel)

scenario.title: Troubleshooting a Failed Deployment to a Static Host
Fixing a Broken CI Pipeline After a Code Change
Responding to a Production Performance Alert

science.desc: Your deployed web application is experiencing a sudden spike in error rates and slow response times. You need to use monitoring tools to identify the root cause and implement a quick fix.

modules.title: Case Studies
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are important for developing critical thinking about real-world systems, learning from existing solutions, and understanding the trade-offs involved in architectural decisions.

skills.title: Analyzing Real-World Web Architectures
lessons: Understanding Data Flow in Complex Systems

scenario.title: Proposing a Frontend Architecture for a New Social Media Feature
Choosing Between SSR and CSR for a Content-Heavy Site
Preventing a Repeat of a Major Frontend Outage

modules.title: Capstone & Career Readiness
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills are paramount for successfully navigating the job search process, from showcasing work to performing well in interviews.

scenario.title: Tailoring Your Portfolio for a Specific Job Application
Optimizing Your Resume for an ATS
Handling a Challenging Live Coding Problem

science.desc: You are asked to design a simple 'To-Do List' application during a live coding interview. Explain your thought process, data structures, and how you would handle user interactions and persistence.
